LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NAGUSSE ZAWDIE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT NAGUSSE ZAWDIE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THE PARTIES OFFER CONFLICTING DECLARATIONS ON MANY POINTS, CERTAIN FACTS ARE NOT DISPUTED. DEFENDANT WAS OUT OF THE COUNTRY WHEN THE LIEN WAS PERFECTED AND COMMUNICATIONS CEASED AT THAT POINT. PLAINTIFF DID NOT NOTIFY DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL WHEN SEEKING THE DEFAULT. THE LAW FAVORS A TRIAL ON THE MERITS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM)

calendar.matters:
Ntc And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-04-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
William F. Abbott, Esq. (408-286-6556), appearing for defendant / moving party NAGUSSE ZAWDIE
J. Miguel Suarez, Esq. of LAW OFFICES OF RAYMOND H. LEVY, INC. 415-753-8080, appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Byrne

Ruling:
DEFENDANT NAGUSSE ZAWDIE'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT NAGUSSE ZAWDIE'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment is granted. Although the parties offer conflicting declarations on many points, certain facts are not disputed. Defendant was out of the country when the lien was perfected and communications ceased at that point. Plaintiff did not notify defendant's counsel when seeking the default. The law favors a trial on the merits. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/VC)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2008 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Jan-17-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Leticia Perez, #7782.
Dennis Soto, Esq. appeared with for Plaintiff: James Byrne a/k/a Jim Byrne, dba J & A Construction.
Plaintiff was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by Defendant Negusse Zawdie aka Nick Zawdie.
Defendant's Default entered - 12/12/07.
Doe Defendants dismissed - 01/17/08.

After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant: Negusse Zawdie aka Nick Zawdie as follows:
Damages/Principal $52,411.00
Prejudgment Interest $5,327.56
Costs $844.75, for a total of $58,583.31.
Furthermore, the Court Ordered that the Mechanic's Lien recorded 03/16/07 is foreclosed.
Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
